violations.explanation: Inline page.locator() wrapping a POM elements reference. The print_history_card locator exists in PrintHistoryPage as a direct property (found in mapping report). Should be accessed directly as print_history_page.print_history_card, not wrapped in page.locator().

violations.expected_code:
# Access via page.locator() with elements string selector is correct for new locators
# But should verify element doesn't exist using expect().not_to_be_visible() instead of count()
expect(page.locator(print_history_page.elements.plan_pause_info)).not_to_be_visible(timeout=30000)
violations.explanation: Using .count() to check element absence. While page.locator(elements.X) is correct for Elements-based string selectors, the verification pattern should use expect().not_to_be_visible() instead of count() for consistency with framework patterns.

fix_details.find:
plan_pause_count = page.locator(print_history_page.elements.plan_pause_info).count()
            assert plan_pause_count == 0, f"Plan pause info should not be visible, found {plan_pause_count} elements"

violations.explanation: Second occurrence - same violation as V001. Inline page.locator() wrapping existing POM property.
This is actually CORRECT for Elements-based string selectors. Marking as false positive - removing from violations.

fix_details.note: This pattern is correct for Elements class string selectors

violations.explanation: The mapping report shows print_history_card exists as a direct locator property in PrintHistoryPage (found in batch 13). It should be accessed as print_history_page.print_history_card, not via page.locator(elements.X). The .elements. namespace is only for string selectors stored in the Elements class, not for locator properties.
This is CORRECT for Elements-based string selectors. No violation.

violations.expected_code:
# ADD THESE ATTRIBUTES TO THE EXISTING Elements CLASS IN PrintHistoryPage:

plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
violations.explanation: The page additions file defines a standalone Elements class instead of showing the attributes to add to the existing Elements class. The merge instructions are correct, but the code structure is misleading. Should show only the attribute definitions without the class wrapper, since these will be added to an existing class.

fix_details.description: Remove the standalone class definition and show only the attributes to be added
fix_details.required_structure:
# Add these attributes to the existing PrintHistoryPage.Elements class:

plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"

violations.current_code:
complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"  # Tooltip appears on hover/click of info icon
additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"  # Tooltip appears on hover/click of info icon
violations.explanation: Both tooltip selectors use identical [role='tooltip'] selector, which will cause conflicts when both tooltips exist on the page. Tooltips need unique selectors or additional filtering to distinguish between complimentary and additional pages tooltips.

fix_details.find:
complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"  # Tooltip appears on hover/click of info icon
    additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
    additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"  # Tooltip appears on hover/click of info icon

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace page.locator(print_history_page.elements.print_history_card) with direct property access print_history_page.print_history_card. The mapping report shows print_history_card exists as a direct locator property in PrintHistoryPage, not as an Elements string selector.

Remove or replace TODO comments with actual implementation or explanatory comments. Implement pause_subscription method in GeminiRAHelper or document manual pause requirement. Document visual testing limitation.
Consolidate logging to one concise message per step. Combine multiple related log statements into single descriptive log.
Restructure page additions file to show only the attributes to add, without the standalone class wrapper. Make it clear these are additions to an existing Elements class.
Add unique identifiers to tooltip selectors using :near() or :has-text() pseudo-selectors to distinguish between complimentary and additional pages tooltips.
